content.problemStatement:
You have a Greenhouse of height xxx units (i.e plants with height > xxx cannot be kept inside it) and nnn plants whose initial heights are
h1,h2‚Ä¶,hnh_1, h_2‚Ä¶,h_nh1‚Äã,h2‚Äã‚Ä¶,hn‚Äã. These plants are of a special kind: they grow by 1 unit if watered. You have to keep these plants in the greenhouse for at most ddd days. On the  iii‚Äôth day, you have to water exactly aia_iai‚Äã different plants. You need to output the maximum number of days you can keep all the plants together inside the greenhouse (output ddd if answer > ddd). Of course, you shouldn't water a plant if it has reached the height of xxx.

###Input:


The first line contains the number of test cases TTT.
For each test case:
The first line contains xxx, nnn and ddd.
The next line contains nnn integers denoting h1,h2..hnh_1, h_2 .. h_nh1‚Äã,h2‚Äã..hn‚Äã.
The next line contains ddd integers denoting a1,a2..ada_1, a_2 .. a_da1‚Äã,a2‚Äã..ad‚Äã.
###Output:
For each test case, output a single integer denoting the maximum number of days for which you can keep the plants in the greenhouse.

###Constraints


1‚â§T‚â§51 \leq T \leq 51‚â§T‚â§5
1‚â§x,d,n,hi‚â§1051 \leq x, d, n, h_i  \leq 10^51‚â§x,d,n,hi‚Äã‚â§105
1‚â§ai‚â§n1 \leq a_i \leq n1‚â§ai‚Äã‚â§n
It is guaranteed that hi<xh_i < xhi‚Äã<x initially.
###Sample Input:
1
5 3 7
1 1 2
3 2 1 1 2 1 3

###Sample Output:
6

###Explanation:
Here is a possible answer. These are the heights of plants in different days -

Day 0: 1 1 2

Day 1: 2 2 3

Day 2: 3 3 3

Day 3: 3 3 4

Day 4: 3 4 4

Day 5: 4 4 5

Day 6: 4 5 5

On the 7th day the heights will be 5 6 6 so you can‚Äôt keep all the plants together. You can check that no other way of watering the plants will do any better. Hence the output is 6.
